arts.ttl: Serie A Liveblog inc Inter-Napoli, Roma-Bologna, Atalanta-Udinese

arts.sum: Udinese have won 27 Serie A matches against Atalanta (D28, L25): only against Cagliari have the Friuliani collected more wins (28) in the top-flight. Atalanta have scored 29 goals in Serie A this season: only Bayern Munich (32) and Barcelona (40) have netted more in the big five European leagues in 2024/25.

arts.ttl: De Ketelaere sidelined for Atalanta’s clash with Udinese due to Injury
arts.sum: Atalanta has been dealt a blow ahead of their upcoming Serie A match against Udinese, as Charles De Ketelaere will be unavailable due to injury. The Belgian attacking midfielder has been a key player for Gian Piero Gasperini’s side this season, but will miss Sunday’s fixture due to a muscular problem. Corriere dello Sport report how De Ketelaere has suffered a lesion in his left biceps femoris, forcing him to sit out the weekend’s game. This marks the first time this season that the former AC Milan player will miss a match, having featured in every game across all competitions The post De Ketelaere sidelined for Atalanta’s clash with Udinese due to Injury appeared first on FootItalia.com.

arts.ttl: Serie A | Atalanta v Udinese: Match Preview
arts.sum: The stakes are high for both teams, as Atalanta prepare to face Udinese in a crucial Serie A clash on Sunday. La Dea, buoyed by an impressive run of form, will look to extend their winning streak and solidify their position near the top of the table. Atalanta have been in remarkable form, securing four victories in just two weeks. Their recent triumph over Serie A leaders Napoli, where they clinched a convincing 3-0 victory at Stadio Maradona, has significantly boosted their confidence. Following that, they continued their success in the UEFA Champions League with a win against Stuttgart, where The post Serie A | Atalanta v Udinese: Match Preview appeared first on FootItalia.com.

arts.ttl: Atalanta’s Giorgio Scalvini set for comeback: Gasperini’s plans unfold
arts.sum: Atalanta has faced a challenging six months, marked by injuries within their defensive lineup while simultaneously securing a fourth consecutive Champions League qualification and celebrating a European Cup victory. The team’s defence has struggled, particularly at the beginning of the season when key players like Scalvini, Kolasinac, Djimsiti, Kossounou, Toloi, and Godfrey have all dealt with injuries. This situation forced coach Gian Piero Gasperini to make tactical adjustments, including repositioning players like Ruggeri and de Roon during critical matches. arts.ttl: Udinese Returnee Suffers Another Frustrating Injury Setback
arts.sum: Alexis Sanchez's return to Udinese has been a textbook definition of a nightmare, with the Chilean yet to make his long-awaited debut this season. According to Calciomercato, another injury setback has postponed the 35-year-old's first appearance for the Friulians since rejoining the club in the summer. The post Udinese Returnee Suffers Another Frustrating Injury Setback appeared first on The Cult of Calcio.
